commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c8e6dc7e685ca38c310a5637d6821bfbb58e868d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR lowers the agent read tool’s automatic page budget when a model context window is known and adds a focused small-context regression test.

PR surface: Source +17, Tests +25. Total +42 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows a 16k-context run targets an 8KB budget, but the patch forwards an estimated line limit while the underlying read still truncates a page at the old 32KB default and the wrapper accepts an oversized first page before capping.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] This protected maintainer draft has a narrow blocking correctness finding; the author or a maintainer should revise it or explicitly authorize a focused repair.

target.title: fix(agents): scale read output for small contexts
target.body:
Summary:
- Scale the default read-tool auto paging cap down when the active model advertises a small context window.
- Keep the existing 32KB default when no model context is known, and keep explicit `read(limit=...)` calls authoritative.
- Add a focused regression test for a 16k-context model so large file reads return a small continuation page instead of a large default page.

Verification:
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- `node --import tsx -e '<read small-context smoke>'`
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --idle-timeout 45m --ttl 90m --shell -- "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.adds-claude-style-aliases-schemas-without-dropping-g.test.ts"`
- GitHub draft helper checks: 11 passed, 0 failed; remaining checks skipped/cancelled by draft gating.

Behavior addressed: small/local model turns can lose too much prompt budget to a default file read page even though the runner already knows the model context window.
Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux runner, provider `aws`, lease `cbx_689e0ead09c9`, slug `silver-hermit`, run `run_8a9948eddf31`, branch rebased onto `origin/main` at `e144d1c8d9`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: created an 8,000-line temp file and called `createSandboxedReadTool` once with `modelContextWindowTokens: 16000` and once without model context; then ran the focused read-tool regression file remotely through Crabbox.
Evidence after fix: small-context read returned `smallLen: 4791` with continuation guidance; no-context read kept the existing `baseLen: 51244` / `capped at 32KB` behavior; Crabbox Vitest reported 2 files and 18 tests passed.
Observed result after fix: the small-context read no longer delivers the old large default page, still tells the model how to continue with `offset`, and the focused regression passes remotely.
What was not tested: full repo check. Testbox-through-Crabbox was attempted first, but Blacksmith auth is unavailable in this environment (`not authenticated - run blacksmith auth login first`), so targeted remote proof used the repo AWS Crabbox provider instead.

Related: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
